rawIntent:
[Feature: Build the leave management module — Phase 8: Implement approve and reject use cases]

Add approveLeave and rejectLeave methods to src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts. approveLeave: validate manager role, update LeaveRequest status to Approved, adjust LeaveBalance (move pendingDays to usedDays), send notification via INotificationRepository, audit log. rejectLeave: similar with Rejected status, release pendingDays. Update tests/unit/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.test.ts with tests for these methods. Depends on Phase 7 service file and all repositories.

Phase architecture notes:
Manager actions that mutate state and balances.

Detailed phase architecture (architecture-agent):

## Canonical dependencies for this phase

Note: this list supersedes any dependency names in the intent
text above. Do not emit ambiguity for mismatches.

The per-phase architecture above is the AUTHORITATIVE source
for the dependency list this phase must implement. The exact
set of types, abstractions, and references defined or used by
this phase is:

- ILeaveRepository
- IBalanceRepository
- IEmployeeRepository
- IAuditRepository
- INotificationRepository

- LeaveRequestStatus

## Deferred to later phases
The following concerns are intentionally OUT OF SCOPE for this phase and will be addressed in subsequent phases:
- Phase 9 — Implement cancel leave, discard draft, and balance recalculation: Add cancelLeave and discardDraft methods to src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts
- Phase 10 — Integrate audit logging, RBAC enforcement, and read operations: Modify src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts to inject IAuditService and enforce

successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.ts exports approveLeave(leaveId: string, approverId: string): Promise<LeaveRequest> and rejectLeave(leaveId: string, approverId: string, comment?: string): Promise<LeaveRequest> methods

approveLeave method updates leave request status to Approved, moves pendingDays to usedDays in the corresponding LeaveBalance, creates an audit log entry, and sends a notification to the employee — all within a single database transaction

rejectLeave method updates leave request status to Rejected, reduces pendingDays in the LeaveBalance, creates an audit log entry, and sends a notification to the employee — all within a single database transaction

Both methods validate that the approver has the manager role and is the manager of the requesting employee (using IEmployeeRepository to check role and manager relationship) and throw a ForbiddenError if not
Input validation is performed on leaveId (UUID format) and approverId (UUID format) before processing
All async errors are caught and handled, with no unhandled promise rejections

Unit tests in tests/unit/modules/leave-management/leave-management.service.test.ts cover approve and reject scenarios including successful approval, successful rejection, unauthorized approver, invalid leaveId, and transaction rollback on failure

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces.
Audit records: All state-changing operations write an audit record.
Input validation: Validate all inputs (leaveId, approverId) before processing.
No sensitive data in logs: Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data.
RBAC enforcement: Validate approver has manager role and is manager of the requesting employee.
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections. All async errors are caught and handled.
All operations within a single database transaction.
Dependency direction: leave-management depends on leave, balance, employee, notification, audit; no reverse dependencies.
outOfScope: Phase 10: integrate IAuditService and RBAC enforcement for read operations
Any other leave management features beyond approveLeave and rejectLeave
API endpoints or UI for these actions
Modifications to existing modules (leave, balance, employee, notification, audit) beyond using their repositories
